feature_build: run-scoped model confirmation bound to canonical routing semantics

source_provenance.claim: Routing confirmation hashes are deterministic under YAML formatting and key-order changes.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/routing-confirmation.test.js compares independently ordered routing documents and deterministic 64-character hashes.

source_provenance.claim: Provider, requested model, effort, fallback, and budget changes invalidate prior confirmation.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/routing-confirmation.test.js changes each material field and checks routing_semantics_changed without another provider attempt.

source_provenance.claim: Human confirmation is ledgered before provider execution and can be reused only by the same durable run.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/provider-confirmation-order.test.js checks ledger ordering; routing-confirmation tests cover restart, later stage, and copied-ledger refusal.

source_provenance.claim: Provider output cannot create approval authority.
source_provenance.source: scripts/specflow-runner.cjs modelConfirmationStatus source and scope filters
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/routing-confirmation.test.js inserts a provider_output confirmation-like record and verifies status remains missing.

source_provenance.claim: Status exposes validity, run scope, semantic hash, confirmation time, and invalidation reason without credentials.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/routing-confirmation.test.js checks valid and invalidated status projections and cost/accounting separation.

notes: Confirmation is authorization for one run and routing semantic hash. It does not authenticate a provider and stores no provider credentials.
